title: Multi-Stage Attack — Full Kill Chain
story: CRITICAL: Threat intelligence correlation has linked multiple low-severity
alerts from the past 6 hours into a single coordinated attack campaign.
Indicators span: web application logs, authentication logs, network firewall,
system audit trails, and endpoint detection events.
You are looking at a full intrusion lifecycle:
Reconstruct the full attack chain. This is your hardest case.
Take your time. Correlate across all log sources.

objectives.label: Identify the initial attack vector (how access was first gained)
What CVE or vulnerability class was exploited for initial access?
What was the final exfiltration destination IP?

hints: Start with the web/apache logs — initial access came through a web application
After web access, look at auth logs and audit logs for what happened next
The attack occurred within a 6-hour window starting around 22:00
Trace the attacker's IP through each stage: initial → pivot → exfil
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case10_* | sort _time | table _time, sourcetype, host, _raw | head 200
